I'm impressed! Overall, Potion Punch offers an excellent gameplay experience reminiscent of games like Pop Cap's Diner Dash! If you've been looking for a game that combines frantic actions, quick decision making, and tough-but-fair gameplay, you found it! The game mechanics are straight forward, though they gain complexity after only a short amount of gameplay. My only major critique is the employment of micro transactions. If it were a flat-rate to play the game, I'd be much happier. 4.8/5

Potion Punch is an amazing fast-paced customer-serving medieval-themed game. The action is in moving food and drinks before customers lose their patience and leave the shop. Actions consist of grilling food, mixing potions, and decorating cups. Customers and their multitude of orders stack up quick and it's a fun challenge to make the most coins possible. The medieval aesthetics, or the simple nature-based environment and the flute/string-instrument music, results in one awesome experience. 5/5.

The only way you can get gems is through ads, in-app purchases, achievements, and daily challenges. But achievements still don't give you nearly as enough gems, and the challenge rewards don't always consist of gems. I know your company needs to make money but I can guarantee you that you've made more money through ads. Gems are just too much of a commodity.

Good. Ads are not invasive, but useful to get double your value so you can quickly purchase upgrades. Each town has specific goals to meet. Easy to accomplish, but challenging enough to be addicted. I'm a big fan of time management games. I've been playing them for years. This one breaks it down into a really simple (yet complex!) way. Your potions incorporate the three primary colors. I'm still at the first restaurant, but there are are a ton of upgrades and it's not a game that's money-oriented. You can watch one ad to boost (ie. Double the income of your restaurant for 3 days). So it's a great way to beat the pay-for-play format while still taking advantage of bonuses. The game gets hard surprisingly quickly but it's probably one of the best I've come across in this genre. :)

But the controls and graphics work amazingly and there's only a few times 
that I might get annoyed simply because I dragged something to the wrong 
glass, but that happens rarely. Overall, I'll spend hours at a time playing 
this game, and I'm so glad I managed to find it!

Having just completed 51 days in-game, I can safely say that this is one of the most engaging management games I have ever had the pleasure of playing.  The gameplay itself, beginning very simply and ramping up at a very reasonable pace, simply comprises dragging objects around the screen to match a product to a customer. By around day 30, this can already feel quite  hectic, but in a good way. It is oddly thrilling and just as satisfying to get a string of orders correct without them leaving in a huff.  The game's F2P implementation and monetization takes the most ubiquitous route, offering a number of purchase options for the premium currency, as well as the availability of video ads that provide shorter term benefits. None of this feels forced, as (so far) it seems perfectly feasible to go through the game without paying any mind to any of that.  Overall, this is a game that can be played in short 3 minute bursts or, as I have done, for over an hour at a time. If you like managing time and resources to maximize profit while upgrading your assets over time, then this is definitely worth a try..

Would love to give this game a higher score. My issue stems from the use of gems. They are very annoying to get without paying. You get two per add and a new area can cost ten or more. Dev if you're reading this I hope you do something because otherwise this game is great, the graphics are cute and it runs very smoothly on my device.

The gameplay is fun and challenging (as in you have to use your brain rather than mindless tapping) It involves a colour blind mode which places shapes in the potions to allow for easier discernment of which mixtures to make. The only thing it falls down is the ridiculous over pricing of gems. I don't mind paying a small price for a few extra things to enhance gameplay ... but 19.99 for a special pack? I can buy two full, decent games on steam for that ... Nevertheless the gameplay allows progression without paying, it's just one of my gripes with free to play. Tldr - Fun game, pricing a bit iffy.

Really fun and challenging gameplay. I really like to play a game like that that actually has a gameplay mechanic instead of just mindless grinding and waiting mechanics.  Serious issue though: Gems should be earnable through gameplay somehow, when they are essential to progression. If those were just for cosmetic upgrades or timesavers, it would be ok for them to only be available via purchase. Because they are necessary for midgame upgrades and unlocking of new stages, they should be available through gameplay as well somehow. Maybe something like a small chance of getting gems as a tip? As it is, I only give 4 stars because progression without spending money or watching a lot of ads is kind of blocked. (some kind of one time purchase for the game ala mario run would be ok as well in my book)

Very  fun twist on traditional time management games....refreshing style to add to the genre. The concept is innovative and different. Instead of layering sandwiches or milkshakes, you've got to do a little extra thinking  about mixing colors. The achievements are fair. Ability to earn gems by watching videos is great. I wouldn't play in without being able to earn this way.  Only criticisms are that it's hard to tell the difference between some of the mixes when you start adding white. Slight adjustments may hhelp.  Second, the dragging items to customers....I would like the option of tapping instead. For example tap the geccko, then type customer, or something.  Sometimes my finger slips or something and I give the wrong potion to a customer because I was trying to give it to the one next to him.   Overall It's a great game and I'll continue playing for now. Been playing about 5 days ish and I've made it to the third place. I went back to the first place and played to 100 so I could get the perfectionist achievement. Played to day 50 in the iron section. We'll see how long it holds my interest!

Potion punch isn't like any other game. Usually whenever you need to upgrade stuff in games like these, you would need to use real money to buy it. However, the creators of Potion Punch has the player as their main goal, instead of pushing us to use our money to go far in this game. Instead of buying gems, you just watch short ads and easily gain gems this way. There's about a 10 ad limit but after a short time, you can watch the ads and just quickly gain gems to use towards upgrades. I just love how there's achievements in the game and that you get rewarded just for completing it. All in all, this is an awesome game

I really love this game because of the graphics, music, game play and easy controls that allow you to pick up and play quickly. The two main reasons I like it so much are that you can play as long as you want without having to wait for "lives"  or anything like that, and the way they incorporate ads into the game is the best way I've seen it done so far. You get to choose whether or not you want to watch the ads, but I always do because it doubles your coins at the end of each level! This means I'm not frustrated and annoyed with pop up ads or banner ads and I get a reward and can move through the game with more coins all because I watched an ad. The one thing I don't like as other people have said is you can't redo a day of game play,  but it doesn't really matter usually as it's not linked to an essential story line or anything like that. The only thing I'm worried about is running out of levels to play! Love it.

Love this game! Cute graphics, very colorful, great music, easy controls, good challenge without being too stressful, and no energy cost, so I can keep on playing as long as I want! Tons of upgrades give it a steady pace in an upward direction, so it gets easier and more fun as I go! LOVE the special guests! I'm won't give any spoilers, but some of them I recognized and was thrilled at the references! I love watching ads for gems; I appreciate being given the choice to watch ads instead of being forced to, and I'll gladly watch them for rewards! I do have one complaint: sometimes when I'm dragging a mixing cup to a spout, it'll snag on the spout next to it and I'll end up pouring the wrong color. Same thing when I'm dragging a drink to a glass; I might have the right drink, but it'll snag on the glass next to it, so I end up pouring it in the wrong place. It's a little annoying, but I can work with it well enough, so I still really love this game! I'm so looking forward to future updates!

Here are a couple gripes I have with the game: 1. I find some of the colors to be overly similar. Dark red and dark orange both look like brown. I find myself having to double check each time which it is. 2. I do not like how going from one village to the next some locations of items change (for example snake versus butterfly). All of the myself memory the player develops early has to be relearned to account for items changing locations. It kind of ruins the satisfying nature of the game if I have to double check for a couple days to make sure I'm grabbing the right thing. 3. I wish you could queue bowls into cups. It takes away the snappy feeling of fulfilling orders when you have to wait for each bowl to finish. Maybe if you could upgrade the pour rate into cups as well as into bowls?

Working for a fast food chain is total agony. I absolutely hate it. I used to not care if I showed up late for my shift. I used to not have any motivation to get up in the morning. I used to freeze up when things got too hectic and would have several panic attacks.  But then I found this game. I played a game once just like this that was a bit more realistic, but it never made me feel as stressed as I do at work. Potion Punch's complexity matches the complexity of working at drive-thru by yourself, having to expedite orders, prepare drinks, drop food, and appease customers alone, on a long and busy day. Playing this game at times makes me freeze up, like I do at work. But playing this game, because it matches my job so well, is great way to practice to stay calm and stay focused during a rush. Now I can get through the day, thanks to you guys!

This game is a lot of fun and is a must play if you have good reflexes.  The mixing is a very well thought out mechanic and once potions start getting layered the game gets hectic, with constantly touching and sliding things to the right places.  The difficulty curve is incredibly well done and I never found the game to be too difficult OR too boring. New mechanics are introduced in every stage and you have just enough time to get used to them.  If I had any gripes It'd have to be the controls, as sometimes I find myself accidentally mixing the wrong potion when I try to set down a cup. Also in the later boss potions some colors look WAY too similar, like brown and dark red.  All in all, this game is a MUST and has given me hours of enjoyment.

This is a fun little time-killer of a game. It's got a fast pace that keeps you focused, though it gets repetitive really fast. It's nice that it's not strictly pay-to-play; you can spend money if you're impatient for upgrades, but there's a watch-ads feature that gets the job done at a slower pace. The one criticism I have for the controls is that it's easy to select the wrong potion or patron while trying to keep up a brisk pace. I think it could be easily fixed by adding a feature that highlights the potion/cup/etc that you're dropping the item at. It already does a kind of size increase sort of thing, but it's so subtle that it can be easily overlooked. Something more noticable would be better.

It's not dependent on a social network sonic can play even though I don't have FB.  The music is full form to the era the game is set to, so I can play w the volume unlike other games. This part is a "game-changer" for me since I enjoy using all senses when playing, all the characters have an individual greeting, it adds to the fun.  THANK YOU for this highly addictive and self competitive game.

I'm often jaded by most games here, but this one has left me feeling a lot better than most here! This time management game is simple but exciting by picking up in speed and complexity quickly after the short tutorial.  It also never forces you to stop because of an energy mechanic or forced ads. In fact ads seem entirely optional! They're suggested for doubling end-of-stage loot, but are never mandatory.   One minus is that you might find it too long to get from one world to the next for changes in scenery and challenges, but overall I recommend this mobile game more than most!

This is a great game for people tired of free games that aren't really free. No timers, no rolling dice, and the game gives you a lot of premium currency for playing. The game itself is challenging and fun, with a steady increase in complexity. Thought this was going to be a typical time management/cooking game, tried it since the art style reminded me of Gravity Falls (which I adore). Plus ads are minimal, and/or optional. I actually spent money on some booster packs, and I NEVER do that with games, EVER. But I am having so much fun that I WANTED to pay!! :)

The gameplay is a fantastic sequel to Potion Punch, but I have two gripes. 1) the game chews up battery like crazy & 2) game energy (mana) quickly becomes a tightly limited hinderance. Lvl. 31, Shroudland Shrooms store and a max mana pool of 37. Each try at level 31 costs 10 mana. A full mana pool allows 3 attempts, and regeneration is 1 mana/10 minutes. Fantastic game! Unlike other games, nothing is hidden behind a paywall. Energy use is optional, so there's no limit to how many levels you can play, and the currencies in the game are easy enough to come by that you can get through the whole game without feeling like you have to spend money. My only warning is this game is VERY fast paced, probably not for people looking for a more leisurely experience. Other than that, this is definitely a shining example of what phone games should be!
